contributor_note: Audit draft for existing Windowsill entry WSL-0005. Botanical identity as Ocimum tenuiflorum is plausible and retained, with Ocimum sanctum treated as a synonym/trade-source issue requiring review. Holy Basil/Tulsi is distinct from sweet basil and Thai basil; some sources also use holy basil loosely for other Ocimum species, so identity should be checked. Edibility is limited to normal culinary leaf/tea use. Temperature and timing values are modelling estimates based on general basil-growing sources plus holy-basil-specific gardening sources; expert review is still pending.

habit.note: Warm-season basil relative that needs strong light and warmth. Windowsill fit is acceptable only with enough sun/heat; balcony and garden fit are good in frost-free warm conditions.

notes: Also known as Tulsi. Distinct from sweet basil and Thai basil. Leaves are used in normal culinary and tea contexts in some cuisines and traditions. Treat as warmth-demanding and frost-tender. This edible-plant entry does not cover supplements, extracts, essential oil, medicinal dosing or health claims.
